steps.title: Finding the vertex location using the formula
steps.content:
All four choices are upward-opening parabolas (leading coefficient 2 > 0). The vertex x-coordinate is found using $x = -\frac{b}{2a}$.

For choices A and B (where $b = -11$):
$$x = -\frac{-11}{2(2)} = \frac{11}{4} = 2.75$$
For choices C and D (where $b = -5$):
$$x = -\frac{-5}{2(2)} = \frac{5}{4} = 1.25$$
From the scatterplot, the minimum occurs near $x = 3$, which is much closer to 2.75 than to 1.25. This eliminates choices C and D.

steps.title: Testing a visible point to distinguish A from B
steps.content:
The rightmost visible point on the scatterplot is approximately at $(5, 15)$.

Testing choice A: $y = 2x^2 - 11x - 20$
$$y = 2(5)^2 - 11(5) - 20 = 50 - 55 - 20 = -25$$
This gives $y = -25$, which does not match the scatterplot value of 15.

Testing choice B: $y = 2x^2 - 11x + 20$
$$y = 2(5)^2 - 11(5) + 20 = 50 - 55 + 20 = 15$$
This matches the scatterplot exactly. That matches choice B — B is correct.
